_: WKNDR TASTE CORPUS — Ness's judgments, injected into every judge (editor + vision). Grows with each curation round. v1 = Board round 1, 2026-07-02, 86 verdicts. v2 = Board round 2, 2026-07-03, 115 verdicts (20 generic club nights killed; 22 canon approvals → src/data/picks.canon2.ts). v3 = Board round 3, 2026-07-04, 81 verdicts (★4+KILL fatigue class → `rested`; organ-concert veto reversed).
imageRules: Prefer atmosphere WITH people in the real place over empty rooms or generic scenery — a market mid-shop, a festival crowd at golden hour, a terrace in use.
For an artist or performer: their actual work or a genuine photo of them — never a stand-in, never another venue.
For theatre/shows: a cast or performance photo beats the venue and beats the poster.
Editorial/official-quality photography preferred; anything that reads as generic stock is bad even when technically on-subject.
No odd crops — the subject must survive the tall portrait frame.
Designed posters with pictorial imagery are fine; text-only posters, logos and wordmarks are not.
Never a different identifiable person, never another venue's recognizable landmark.

imageExemplars.why: generic-looking image — flagged in BOTH rounds; in R2 it killed the whole event
chose the festival crowd in the park over a generic shot
chose the floating market seen from the water — the thing that makes it singular
R4: wrong image two rounds running — the flyer shown is not this night’s; prefer the RA flyer for the EXACT event id, else the venue’s own photo

eventRules: Community-authentic gatherings are NOT filler: neighbourhood singing sessions, maker markets, museum markets, open-air food culture score WELL when they are genuinely local and warm.
Food culture is core WKNDR: supper clubs, chef one-offs, food-film mashups, brunch institutions rate high.
Design/craft retail with a strong point of view (concept stores, ateliers, specialist shops) rates high as weekend material.
Quirky-unique formats (a lying-down concert, cinema-dinner hybrids) are exactly the surprising texture the deck needs.

KILL on sight: tribute acts and cover shows; VR/game/experience museums; branded comedy-franchise nights; church lunch concerts; generic revue/cabaret listings; arthouse reruns with no occasion; wide-release cinema.

Club nights need a curatorial identity to ship: BRET- or POISED-class programming passes; generic weekly party formats — 'X All Night Long' residencies, hip-hop/R&B/latin 'vibes' nights, lineup-dump hard-techno bills — are filler even at good venues (Ness killed 20 of them in one round).
Judge the event, not the address: a beloved venue does not carry a weak event (Hortus Botanicus is ★5; its Zomeravonden listing was killed).
A pick is only as good as its card: a generic image can kill an otherwise fine event (Homelanding died on its image two rounds running).
Institutional museum wallpaper (a permanent collection with no distinct show) ranks low; a distinct exhibition with strong imagery ranks high.
Recurring is fine when it is beloved and local (a weekly market, a legendary club night); recurring is filler when it is a venue's self-promo.

_vetoNote: Taste kills from the board — dropped at publish, every run, any source; matched with WORD BOUNDARIES (refresh.ts) so short names like 'monne' can't hit 'Monnickendam'. Dupe-kills are deliberately NOT here: R1's Julidans/TREK/Vondelpark twins, and R2's 'The Netherlands – Japan: Connected Through Time' (EN twin of the ★4 Dutch-titled show) were dedup actions, not taste. R3 REVERSAL: 'lunchtime organ concert' removed — Ness ★4'd the Waterlooplein series (community-authentic wins); the Westerkerk lunchconcerten veto stands.

rested.note: ★4 + KILL (R3) — likes it, seen it three weekends straight
★4 + KILL (R3, issue #3) — likes it, tired of the market cycle; image also swapped for its return
R3 issue #3 KILL — a TREK variant the 'festival trek' match misses; same fatigue class

_restedNote: The ★4+KILL class from the board: events Ness LIKES but is tired of seeing. NOT a veto — dropped from the feed AND the bench only until their `until` date (then they may return fresh). Their starAnchors and starredKeeps stay: the taste signal is intact, only the exposure rests. Dropped AFTER the starredKeeps pass so carry-forward can't resurrect them.
_starredNote: ★4-5 live events from the board: (a) editorScore floor 8 when present, (b) CARRIED FORWARD from the prior feed when an adapter fails to re-surface them and they're still date-valid — Ness's investment must not evaporate between runs. Matched with word boundaries (so 'bak' ≠ 'bakkerij'). R2 removals: 'oatly x radio radio' (reversed to KILL — now vetoed).
_topPicksNote: R3 issue #3 👑 escalations. Milkshake + Dekmantel are future festivals (late Jul) — TOP is armed and activates when they enter the feed. 't Lemmeke = current restaurant. ARTIS: Ness starred the bench 'ARTIS-Aquarium' card (weak kidsproof.nl link); routed to the canon 'ARTIS Royal Zoo' entry (same visit, real artis.nl link, guaranteed availability) so TOP actually sticks.
_topNote: Ness's 👑 TOP escalations from the board — the tier ABOVE starredKeeps. Matched picks (word-boundary, any source incl. canon via feed.topMatches) get editorScore 10, lead the served deck ahead of everything, and carry the 'Top pick' pill. Keep this list SHORT (≤6) — if everything is top, nothing is.
